rulings.Case Title: FIRST SELECT CORPORATION vs. CHEN, WEN LE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption And Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 3, 2014, Line 22, ASSIGNEE SUNLAN LDP, LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption of Wen Lie. Denied in part. Claimant Wen Lie has not demonstrated that all funds are necessary for the support of the claimant, spouse and family. Plaintiff's judgment takes priority over other debt, and the claimed transportation expenses are excessive in light of the pending judgment. Wages will be garnished in the amount of $300 per pay period. rulings.Case Title: GARRETT PROCKNOW VS. WABASH METAL PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages As To Deft Wabash Metal Products, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 3, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF GARRETT PROCKNOW's Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint To Add Punitive Damages As To Defendant Wabash Metal Products, Inc. The motion is denied. The Court sustains Defendant's objection to the "amended" motion papers filed without leave of court subsequent to the opposition papers. Plaintiff's motion as filed fails to comply with CRC 3.1324(b) and is denied on that basis. Plaintiff's declaration does not specifically highlight which discovery he obtained that gave rise to the proposed amendment or why the motion could not have been made earlier. Moreover, Plaintiff delayed several months in bringing the motion, and allowing amendment now in light of the current trial date would unduly prejudice defendant. rulings.Case Title: PETER WOODROW et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order 1) Compelling Pltf Charles Woodrow To Submit To An Independent Psychological Examination 2) Compelling Pymt Of Cancellation Fee To Dr. Joanna Berg, And 3) Moving For Sanctions For Abuse Of The Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, March 3, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and PATSY THOMAS' Motion For Order 1) Compelling Plaintiff Charles Woodrow To Submit To An Independent Psychological Examination 2) Compelling Payment Of Cancellation Fee To Dr. Joanna Berg, And 3) Moving For Sanctions For Abuse Of The Discovery Process. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part and denied in part. CCSF's Motion to Compel Charles Woodruff to Submit to an Independent Psychological Examination is granted unless plaintiff stipulates in writing that he will not present an expert witness in support of his claim for negligent infliction of emotional distress. If plaintiff will not so stipulate, then the motion is granted subject to the parties first meeting and conferring in an effort to narrowly tailor the exam's scope. Mr. Woodruff's attorney may not be present at the exam absent the express written agreement of defendant or a showing of good cause to the court at the hearing of this motion as to why he should be allowed to be present. The court does not have jurisdiction over Dr. Berg, but directs the City to ask her to invoice Mr. Woodruff and his attorney directly for her $900 cancellation fee less amounts, if any, by which she was able to mitigate lost income. If plaintiff and his attorney fail to pay the (mitigated) cancellation fee, and the City ends up paying it, then plaintiff and his attorney are ordered to reimburse the City for its actual out of pocket expense in this regard. Sanctions are denied on the Motion to Compel. Even though plaintiff voluntarily appeared, however briefly, at the mental exam, CCP section 2032.310 mandates leave of court before obtaining such discovery. There was no prior leave of court here. No sanctions are awarded for abuse of the discovery process. However if plaintiff and his attorney fail to fully cooperate with the mental exam, then, given the evidence in the record here, sanctions will likely be awarded on further motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PERCY-MARIE LACY VS. SAN FRANCISCO ZOOLOGICAL SOCIETY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Compendium
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 3, 2014, Line 21, PLAINTIFF PERCY-MARIE LACY's Motion For Statutory Attorneys Fees And Compendium. Fees are awarded in the amount of $444,752. The Court has carefully reviewed the pleadings and declarations and has determined that the number of hours reasonably spent should be reduced by 70 (or 7.82%), largely attributable to some of the time spent in intra-office conferences, motion practice, and some other miscellaneous tasks that would reasonably have been accomplished in less time. This reduction has been spread proportionally across the three attorneys' total time. The Court, having reviewed the evidence concerning prevailing rates, concludes that reasonable hourly rates for purposes of this calculation for Mr. Becht should be $500, for Mr. Sachs, $430, for Ms. Sivarajah, $350, and for Ms. Watson, $150. The Court will not conclude that plaintiff's counsel should not have undertaken any of the tasks on which they spent time or that they should have delegated tasks among themselves differently. Those and other challenged issues are within the purview of counsel, and the Court finds no reason to second guess them. The resulting lodestar amount is $370,627 after rounding calculations to whole dollars. Recognizing the contingent, risky nature of the litigation and the importance of encouraging attorneys to undertake FEHA litigation, as well as the reductions in rates to what the Court concludes are reasonable if the litigation were compensated on an hourly basis, the Court will apply a multiplier of 1.2 to the lodestar to arrive at the figure awarded above. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: DALE C WEBB VS. 3234 WASHINGTON STREET ASSOCIATES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Webbs Fourth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief To Amend Judgment To Add The Storey'S As Judgment Debtors And Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, March 3, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs WILL STOREY and VERLE STOREY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Webb's Fourth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief To Amend Judgment To Add The Storeys As Judgment Debtors And Evidence. Granted. Defendants William Storey and Verle Storey have met their initial burden. Defendants cannot be added to the prior judgment as alter egos because they did not control the underlying litigation-indeed they had no knowledge of it until the filing of this subsequent action. (NEC Electronics Inc. v. Hurt (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 772, 781. See UMF no. 5.) Plaintiff has not met her shifting burden. Plaintiff's argument that she need not show control of the underlying litigation when a subsequent action is filed is not supported by the legal authority cited. " ' "[A] point which is merely suggested by [a party's] counsel, with no supporting argument or authority, is deemed to be without foundation and requires no discussion." [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Do It Urself Moving & Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 35, superseded by statute on other grounds in Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 582.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
